evals.prompt: I need to add a 'FermentationNote' entity to the fermentation module. It should let winemakers attach a free-text note to any fermentation, with a timestamp and the user who wrote it. Walk me through implementing it end-to-end.
evals.expected_output: Full TDD implementation: reads context hierarchy first (module-context.md, domain-model-guide.md), writes failing tests first, then implements domain entity (extends BaseEntity with winery_id), DTO, repository interface, repository implementation, service interface, service, API router, and Alembic migration. Absolute imports throughout. context-maintenance checklist applied at the end.
expectations: Reads context hierarchy (project-context.md, module-context.md) before writing any code and reports what already exists
Writes failing tests first (TDD Red) and runs them before implementing
Uses absolute imports (from src.modules.fermentation...) throughout all generated code
Actually runs the test suite and reports results (pass/fail counts)
Updates .ai-context/module-context.md and project-context.md with new component and test counts
Runs the full suite (run_all_tests.ps1 or equivalent) to confirm no regressions

evals.prompt: We want a completely new module called 'notifications' that sends alerts when fermentation anomalies are detected. What's the setup process before I write any code?
evals.expected_output: Creates all context files first using the templates: module-context.md (Template 1), domain-model-guide.md (Template 2), adds entry to project-context.md (Template 4), PROJECT_STRUCTURE_MAP.md (Template 6), ADR-INDEX.md. Then identifies pyproject.toml, Dockerfile, conftest.py, and docker-compose.yml changes needed. Does NOT write implementation code yet.
expectations: Creates .ai-context/module-context.md from Template 1 before any code
Does not write entity/service code until context is set up

evals.prompt: The analysis engine's AnomalyDetectionService is getting too large. It's doing anomaly detection AND recommendation generation AND protocol integration all in one class. What should we do?
evals.expected_output: Pushes back critically (per collaboration-principles.md), identifies SRP violation, proposes splitting into 3 focused services each with its own interface, explains how to introduce the change safely (new interface first, extract via TDD, keep existing tests green). References NIVEL 3 context (analysis engine module-context.md) to understand current implementation status before proposing changes.
expectations: Reads analysis_engine module-context.md before proposing any changes and reports what it found
Does not blindly accept the premise — verifies if the stated violation actually exists in the real code
If the premise is wrong, says so clearly with evidence from the context files
If a real design problem exists, proposes a concrete alternative with interfaces
References SOLID principles (SRP, OCP) by name when explaining the diagnosis
